PUH-BrianMartell VASCO Project Details- Direct PUH Link The VASCO project (full name: Vanishing and Appearing Sources during a Century of Observations) is a real, ongoing international citizen-science astronomy project led by Beatriz Villarroel (Nordic Institute for Theoretical Physics, Sweden) and collaborators from Spain, USA, Ukraine, and India. Core Method They compare old photographic plates from the 1950s (US Naval Observatory Catalogue USNO B1.0 — ~600 million objects) with modern digital surveys (Pan-STARRS DR1 and later data releases). Any object visible in the 1950s plates but completely gone today is a “vanishing source.” Key Findings (2020–2026) •  Initial search (Villarroel et al. 2020, The Astronomical Journal): ~150,000 preliminary candidates missing in modern images. •  Visual inspection of ~24,000 candidates revealed ~100 genuine point sources that appeared only in the old red-band plates and vanished forever. •  Citizen-science expansion (2017–2025)
